claim: The muon magnetic anomaly shows a 4.2-sigma discrepancy with the Standard Model
status: resolved by theory revision, not by the measurement: final Fermilab a_mu = 116592070.5(11.4)(9.1)e-11 (127 ppb) vs Theory Initiative WP25 SM 116592033(62)e-11 gives exp minus SM = 38(63)e-11, about 0.6 sigma

claim: The W boson is about 7 sigma heavier than the Standard Model fit (CDF II 80433.5 +/- 9.4 MeV)
status: failed replication at matched precision: CMS measured 80360.2 +/- 9.9 MeV, agreeing with the SM fit, ATLAS, and LHCb; the CDF value stands unexplained but isolated, about 5.4 sigma from CMS
sigma_now_note: sigma_now is CDF-vs-CMS, not CDF-vs-SM; the anomaly claim itself did not replicate

claim: Cosmic shear measures S8 2-3 sigma below the Planck prediction (lensing is low)
status: dissolved: KiDS-Legacy S8 = 0.815 +0.016/-0.021 sits 0.73 sigma from Planck, a 2.2-sigma decrease vs the prior KiDS release, with about two thirds of the reduction from redshift-calibration methodology rather than new sky data

claim: Two experiments disagree about the Hubble constant at 5.8 sigma (SH0ES vs Planck)
status: the gap persists (73.17 +/- 0.86 vs 67.4 +/- 0.5, 5.8 sigma) but the popular two-experiments frame is wrong in shape: JWST-calibrated CCHP ladders land between (TRGB 70.39, JAGB 67.80, from arXiv:2408.06153), Cepheid crowding is excluded as the cause at 8 sigma, and the disagreement lives in calibrator and supernova-sample choices

claim: DESI has discovered evolving dark energy
status: below discovery threshold: DR2 BAO prefers w0waCDM over LCDM at 3.1 sigma with CMB, 2.8 to 4.2 sigma with supernovae depending on compilation; a model-selection statement, not an interval non-overlap, and possibly absorbable by supernova systematics

claim: The e+e- hadronic cross-section data give a single reliable dispersive HVP average
status: unresolved at the field level: the CMD-3 vs KLOE/BaBar tensions forced WP25 to drop the data-driven HVP average entirely; no experiment has been shown wrong; the consensus chose cannot-combine over a forced average
